task_completion.description: Log into the OpenMRS Administration interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS Administration dashboard is visible, indicating successful login using the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Relationship Types management page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Manage Relationship Types' or 'Add Relationship Type' page is visible in the OpenMRS interface.

task_completion.description: Enter the 'A is to B' name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Community Health Worker' is entered into the 'A is to B' input field on the relationship type creation form.

task_completion.description: Enter the 'B is to A' name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Client' is entered into the 'B is to A' input field on the relationship type creation form.
The text 'Links a community health worker to their assigned client for ongoing care coordination and follow-up' is entered into the Description text area.
A success message is displayed (e.g., 'Relationship Type saved'), or the new relationship type 'Community Health Worker/Client' is visible in the list of existing relationship types.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with the OpenMRS GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser interface navigating the OpenMRS web application, not a text editor or script.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands or API requests to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing curl commands, database queries (SQL), or automated scripts creating the relationship type.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with genuine form fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) are not used to fake the presence of the relationship type in the HTML DOM.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected 'A is to B' name is 'Community Health Worker'. The expected 'B is to A' name is 'Client'. The expected description is exactly 'Links a community health worker to their assigned client for ongoing care coordination and follow-up'. The description must contain the keywords: community, health, worker, client, care, coordination.
